sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: Admissions | New Student Onboarding | Advising | Curriculum | Honors | Minor in Criminal Justice | Student Learning Outcomes | Student Resources | Career Opportunities
sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.rawLine: Offered in both on-campus and online formats, the Criminal Justice degree offers a multidisciplinary understanding of crime and justice within the framework of broader social processes within our society. It looks at all major aspects of the justice system from an ecological systems approach rather than a specialization in one narrow content area. The curriculum provides a theoretical understanding of the discipline, combined with an understanding of the scientific method as it applies to criminal justice.

sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.rawLine: UW Tacoma's innovative Criminal Justice major emphasizes community partnerships, systems thinking and skill development. Students are sensitized to the human impact of crime, including differential impact across social groups and locations. Students will also become familiar with a variety of rehabilitative and restorative approaches to crime and justice.
